title: A Bull in the Passenger Seat

source.title: Car with giant bull as passenger pulled over by Nebraska police

segments.title: Police Receive a Call
segments.audioText: Around 10 a.m., the Norfolk Police division responded to a call of a man driving eastbound on 275 with a Watusi bull in his passenger seat.
segments.transcript: Around 10 a.m., the Norfolk Police division responded to a call of a man driving eastbound on 275 with a Watusi bull in his passenger seat.
segments.translationZh: 大約早上十點，Norfolk 警察部門接到報案，話有個男人喺 275 號公路向東行，乘客位坐住一隻 Watusi 牛。

points.label: Around 10 a.m.
points.explanationZh: around 表示『大約』；新聞開頭常先交代時間。

points.label: responded to a call
points.explanationZh: responded to 即係『回應、處理』；a call 喺呢度係報警電話。

points.label: driving eastbound on 275
points.explanationZh: eastbound 係『向東行』；on 275 指喺 275 號公路上。

points.label: in his passenger seat
points.explanationZh: passenger seat 係乘客位；聽到呢個位就知道新聞有幾特別。

segments.title: What Officers Expected
segments.audioText: They thought it was going to be, you know, like a calf, something smaller, something that actually fit inside the vehicle.
segments.transcript: They thought it was going to be, you know, like a calf, something smaller, something that actually fit inside the vehicle.
segments.translationZh: 佢哋以為會係，好似一隻小牛，一啲細啲、真係放得入架車嘅動物。

points.label: They thought it was going to be
points.explanationZh: 呢段係講警員原本嘅預期；going to be 連住聽，唔好逐隻字停。

points.label: you know, like a calf
points.explanationZh: you know 係口語填充；like a calf 即『好似一隻小牛』。

points.label: something smaller
points.explanationZh: something + adjective 係常見講法，意思係『某樣較細嘅嘢』。

points.label: actually fit inside the vehicle
points.explanationZh: fit inside 指『放得入入面』；vehicle 係較正式嘅『車輛』。

segments.title: Driver and Bull Identified
segments.audioText: The occupant of the vehicle was identified as Lee Meyer of Neely.
segments.transcript: The occupant of the vehicle was identified as Lee Meyer of Neely.
segments.translationZh: 車入面嘅人被確認係來自 Neely 嘅 Lee Meyer。

points.label: The occupant of the vehicle
points.explanationZh: occupant 係『車內人士、佔用者』；新聞報道比 driver 更正式。

points.label: was identified as
points.explanationZh: 被動式，意思係『被確認為』；新聞常用嚟交代身份。

points.label: Lee Meyer of Neely
points.explanationZh: of Neely 表示『來自 Neely』，地名通常跟喺人名後面。

segments.title: Warnings Instead of Arrest
segments.audioText: The officer wrote him some warnings.
segments.transcript: The officer wrote him some warnings.

points.label: The officer
points.explanationZh: officer 喺新聞入面通常指警員；重音落喺第一音節。

points.label: wrote him
points.explanationZh: wrote him 即『寫畀佢』；him 係受詞，指司機。

points.label: some warnings
points.explanationZh: warnings 係『警告』，唔係正式罰單；some 表示幾項。

segments.title: Heading Home Safely
segments.audioText: Meyer and Howdy Doody are on their way back home, and no one was hurt.
segments.transcript: Meyer and Howdy Doody are on their way back home, and no one was hurt.
segments.translationZh: Meyer 同 Howdy Doody 而家返緊屋企，事件中冇人受傷。

points.label: are on their way back home
points.explanationZh: on their way 係『喺路上』；back home 即返屋企。

points.label: Howdy Doody
points.explanationZh: 呢個係牛嘅名；聽到人名同動物名時，先抓大概聲音。

points.label: no one was hurt
points.explanationZh: 新聞結尾常用句，表示『冇人受傷』，語氣簡短清楚。

sentences: Around 10 a.m., the Norfolk Police division responded to a call of a man driving eastbound on 275 with a Watusi bull in his passenger seat.
They thought it was going to be, you know, like a calf, something smaller, something that actually fit inside the vehicle.
The occupant of the vehicle was identified as Lee Meyer of Neely.
The officer wrote him some warnings.
Meyer and Howdy Doody are on their way back home, and no one was hurt.

vocabulary.example: The police responded to a call.
vocabulary.translationZh: 警方回應咗一個報案電話。

vocabulary.example: The man was driving eastbound on 275.
vocabulary.translationZh: 嗰個男人喺 275 號公路向東行。

vocabulary.example: The bull was in the passenger seat.
They thought it was going to be like a calf.
vocabulary.translationZh: 佢哋以為會係好似一隻小牛。

vocabulary.example: Something smaller could fit inside the vehicle.
vocabulary.translationZh: 細啲嘅嘢先可以放得入架車。

vocabulary.example: The occupant of the vehicle was identified.
vocabulary.translationZh: 車入面嘅人被確認咗身份。

vocabulary.example: He was identified as Lee Meyer.
The officer wrote him some warnings.

phrases.example: The Norfolk Police division responded to a call.
There was a Watusi bull in his passenger seat.
They expected something that could fit inside the vehicle.
The occupant was identified as Lee Meyer.
Meyer and Howdy Doody are on their way back home.

speakingPractice.instructionZh: 由 00:00 開始跟讀第一句，留意 around 10 a.m. 同 responded to a call 之間嘅停頓。
重播 00:18，先寫低你聽到嘅名詞：calf, something smaller, vehicle。
模仿 00:53 嗰句短句節奏：The officer / wrote him / some warnings。
用 2 句英文複述：Police got a call. Meyer and Howdy Doody went back home safely。

notes: 呢條片語速清楚，但有地名、人名同牛嘅名字；第一次盲聽時只抓主線：police, car, bull, warnings, home。
用時間碼逐句重播，先聽完整句，再對照 transcript，最後跟讀同一個節奏。
第二句有 you know 呢類口語填充，聽寫時可以先略過，完成主句之後再補返。
audioText 同 transcript 已按可核對嘅字幕逐句對齊，練習時唔需要背改寫句。
